Had a call back from Rod, evidently he didn't get the below email sent this email sent on 23rd March 2018. So I resent it again.

Hi Rod,
Further to our chat that the Monster Tools Sale event at Silverdale PlaceMakers branch. If you liked the idea of the NextMinute app we would welcome the opportunity to show you more. Based on what we discussed the timesheets, job costs and job management areas would help your business, NextMinute would help you improve process and give you a tool for the guys to efficiently record labour and materials AND we integrate with your PlaceMakers activity for more efficient quoting, ordering and supplier invoices.
We run twice weekly webinars, our next one is Tuesday 27th March at 4:30pm. You can book-in here. This might be a good way for you to get an overview of what's possible with NextMinute.
As I mentioned trying NextMinute is free. You can start a 14-day free trial, during this time our team are available help you get up and running. Following the trial the costs are $28 per admin user/ per month and $9 per mobile user / per month (so this would be your guys on the tools). Spend a dollar, get an hour back! Slash your admin time by 4-5 hours per week with NextMinute. Here's how you start a free trial, learn more. If you want to start a free trial, sign up here.
A quick read....rather be fishing this summer, check this info on how to ditch the paper work. Get your life back.
I hope to speak soon! Have a great weekend.
Best,

Learning exercise call review:

  • Notes and improvements
  • Information gathered
  • Call breakdown

Call length 5mins 29secs

Notes/improvements:

Call length: I think the length of this call was just right, I don't believe the initial call should be much longer than min 5mins max 10mins. We don't want to confuse the prospect.

Opening:

Good: For this call my intro led straight into questions and what Craig is wanting help with.

Improvement: I got quite a bit of information from this call without having to push for too much, for this call try and get more information about how they currently work. All calls - ask how much time they have to talk, if they have some time for a training or demo session, find out how they currently manage their jobs, ask what they struggle with in everyday work life currently.

Q/A:

Good: Discovered pain points, his main NM benefit so far and his overall goal from a job management solution. I didn't go into too much detail as we know it can be quite overwhelming for prospects.

Improvement: Be more confident in my answers, ask how he is managing invoices, timesheets, health and safety currently.

Closing:

I don't believe I should make any changes here, majority of customers say they appreciate knowing help is available.

Information gathered:

Pain Points:

  • Struggles getting all supplier invoices together
  • Getting the teams timesheets
  • Having all job information in one place, doesn't want a number of different apps
  • Health and Safety/ Tool box talk

Benefit of NM: He loves that he can view his PlaceMakers price and the PlaceMakers retail price, this isn't available out of NextMinute.

Goal: Wants a solution that meets all their needs to reduce the number of apps they use

Call breakdown

Opening:

Introduce myself and explain the reason of the call - to see how they're getting on with their trial and if they need any help getting started.

Q/A:

1. How do I import supplier Invoices that aren't from PlaceMakers?

A. We are working on making this easier to do with a csv file

Work around: Currently you can go into a Job, select supplier invoices from the top options, select to add and quick add, add in the supplier, a description, the total, attach the file and save.

2. Is that the same for quotes for big jobs? sub-contractors send their quotes and he sends a total to the client

A. I will send a help article for this as well. You can build a quote using sections for each sub-contractor and add the total for each which can then be sent off the the client in one quote from you. (I was confused with this question at first, he explained straight away for me to answer. Wasn't too sure if the CSV import will allow you to import other contractors quotes to combine into one)

3. How to view or import the PlaceMakers pricebook?

A: We will be moving this to sale items, currently need to go into job charges, quotes, invoices or orders (said you can submit orders with PM in NM), select to add a material and select PlaceMakers as your supplier to search pricebook.

Summary: I will send you through some help articles for the PlaceMakers pricebook, Quick add and quotes.

4.1 Can I try timesheets with some staff?

A: Yes definitely, invite users in using their email (explained the process) and amend their role permissions.

4.2 I will need to have jobs setup?

A: Yes, and you can allow them to view all jobs or just theirs which is user assigned.

5. Does NextMinute have a way to manage health and safety or tool box talk?

A: Not currently although this is something in our development pipeline

Work around: Use Job Sheets, load files and photos against Job

Closing:

Said I will send through help articles and reminded him that we are here to help via phone and email and to enjoy his day.

Send follow up email with help articles

Retail Merchandising, install stands - App to take before and after photos, checklist, order and report to client, time is billable.

- Multiple lead sources (some with PO)

- Quoted and Charge-up, always Labour. Quote based on hourly rates.

- Scheduling done via email, thanks for accepting assignment.

- Contractors invoice

- Employees (merchandisers), log hours in store vs travel time

- Merchandisers once a month. Cyclical every so many weeks. Pay 25, bill 45. Need to add work time as a rate, travel time as a rate, kms, parking etc. Can manage that.

- Contractors only work once every blue moon, so need to manage how those would fit in and if we could handle. Have advised based on his business size and uniqueness, that I would adjust the pricing to suit once we know it will work (500+ staff)

Meeting notes - 25 March 2021

Discovery notes:

  • Level 45 are associated with a global networking group. An associate in the UK was contacted by Andrew Moore and they passed our details along to Eddie.
  • Doing some Niche marketing to target specific industries - Eddie is currently focusing on trades and would like to provide a full end-to-end solution for them.
  • Would like to do things differently too.
  • 1/4 of his revenue is coming from tradies and he would like to triple this.
  • Xero preferred practice.
  • Has built his own internal spreadsheets that have a profit calculator for tradies in it.
  • Looking for a tool that can do quick & easy quotes, has a profit report, and would like it to have payment integration.

Feedback:

  • Generally has a 15 minute rule for using software - if he cannot do it in 15 minutes, it does not pass the test. He did not feel NM passed this test.
  • Would like to see a walk-through in the app.
  • I suggested trying the mobile app, it is simpler to navigate compared to the web version.
  • After giving him a walk-through, he was almost sold on it and said after it was explained, he felt it ticked more of the boxes.
  • Would like "white-labeled" access e.g. have the software presented as "Level 45 Trades" so when his client's login, it looks like it is his. He is very confident in his brand. He would be willing to pay for this - I explained our partner program is new, so this is not something we would be looking at in the near future.

Next steps/questions/follow-up:

  • Verbally accepted being part of the partner program (EM will send a separate email regarding this)
  • EM to convert his sandbox account
  • EM to send the financial report we have
  • Send the best steps for recording progress payments/invoices
  • Send the "set-up checklist" when it is available - he would like to do some set-ups for his own education.

Referral from Hanna Mace at Hidden Asset.

They decided to go with Tradify because of the checklist feature.

I have let Hanna know we will be building this, but no scheduled date as of yet.

Minutes from our chat with Ben

Ben: 1x Block requires 4x different tasks which then means even in that basic little unit, there was 109x tasks or something that we would have to set up. Now to do that in NextMinute, click it, add it, wait for it to save then add the next one, it's just a lot of hours so if we can upload something in there from excel where I can basically copy & paste data because a lot of the tasks have many different points but it's almost consistent in what we have to do

Devon: I'm wondering if, you know how you say 1 block has 3-4 tasks, if you instead added one task per block, but then you had a google drive link or something in the description/notes and that opened up something like a spreadsheet for that block with the 3-4 tasks in it, then you could maybe make a checklist for your users - would that sort of work? It would minimize a lot of manual entry

Ben: I guess what we really want to be able to do is we want to be able to go from the estimation stage, pull all of that through into the Job. Now my understanding is NextMinute sort of works from the Job then spreads out from there, so you'd have to create a Job, then create the Quote which is a little bit backwards to what we're used too

Devon: You can do it the other way as well

Ben: It doesn't bring all of the sections across if you do it the other way we've found. I think we had that conversation earlier on with Tracey, because Tracey who does all of our insurance work was doing exactly that, creating a Quote and then creating a Job and she basically had to have two windows open and copy everything across which seemed crazy but that's when we discovered that yeah it was job based not quote as a first point. But yeah we want to be able to go from that estimation stage and sort of just get the data to flow through which then helps our supervisors schedule because the estimation stage obviously has those scope sections and in those scope sections we want to be able to create those tasks from there. Now to be able to copy something like this thing into Monday.com or, I don't know, any of those other sort of things but they don't integrate with our accounting software either so we've kind of got this are where NextMinute does what we want, but I can't get enough data into it quick enough to make it efficient. Whereas with an excel spreadsheet I can basically go in, enter our scope in there, convert it from PDF to excel, copy & paste it into whatever format and having to go into NextMinute to upload it might take half an hour to do a full scope, whereas at the moment it takes about 5 minutes to do a whole task

Devon: I completely understand that, I imagine it's incredibly frustrating. I can tell you now that we are looking at in the future, I'm not certain of dates or anything at this stage, but say you've got a quote, and you've got your tasks as separate 'sections' in your quote for example, your 'mobile bathroom', your 'skip bin' and 'storage containers' - when you create a Job from this Quote you'll be able to add these 'sections' as tasks against the Job so you don't have to do so much manual entry - obviously this is not helpful for right now, but it's good to know for the future. I'm not 100% sure about how best to go about trying to make uploading these tasks as minimal entry for you as possible.

Ben: Yeah, I suppose the other thing is this is only the tip of the iceberg to for what I need it to be able to do. But you can imagine, this is one Job we're talking about, there's 24x units that we're looking at, and initially we hadn't been using the scheduling side of it just because of the data input side I suppose, we're just trying to look at what we needed to put in versus what we didn't and the boys are literally running a spreadsheet just to create a 'ok this task needs to be done this date' and so on which is only at a trade level which then means they have a tendency to miss sections of the scope because they haven't based it off the actual tendered documents as such, they're just going through and thinking 'okay I've got this bathroom that needs to be done so I'm going to do that bathroom and that bathroom' and so on. Because there's so much detail in these scopes with so many different sections, without a computerized system you can overlook things. If you want to look at the 'Tatura Pergola' job and their tasks that's one I sort of set up as a, that's just one little $1000 construction of a pergola thing so it was sort of my 'work out how to use the system' job - we basically entered the tasks on a 'trade / letter acceptance / initial tasks' type thing, but even in those, realistically there's sub-tasks in that but it was giving us enough information to manage that Job but then when we scale it up to a set of units it's not enough detail to truly keep track of it. It's sort of more like, it's useful as a 'progress tracking' more than an actual 'scheduling' program at that level.

Devon: How many units it there that you're going to have to do?

Ben: That one's 24x units that are all very similar to that excel stage 1 level 1 scope I sent to you

Devon: okay so 1x block of 24x units for examples sake, how many units do you have?

Ben: normally we have, depending on the covid situations, we'd normally have around 200x houses on the go at a time. 200x houses 200x units depending on what they are

Devon: So roughly like 5x blocks?

Ben: It depends, you get some where their say 4x units, in like a little court area, or we might get 65x houses that all just require external renovations, it just depends on how the packages are released

Devon: It seems like the 'stage 1 channel road unit 1' scope that you sent through, are they all sort of the same

Ben: Yeah they're a similar format

Devon: The other thing you could do is template 1x lot of units as a job with tasks, and then duplicate that for all of your other blocks

Ben: into individual jobs you're talking?

Devon: Yes

Ben: The only problem I've got with that is the material side of it I can't split purchase orders between jobs

Duncan: Just on the bills, the supplier invoices that you import just ball park, how many bills do you put on a unit job like this? Is there quite a lot of separate ones or is it just a handful of big ones?

Ben: There are a lot of little bits & pieces, that's probably the biggest problem we're having is because it's renovations, it might be like an $80 order for these bits & pieces and they might come from 15 different suppliers and it might need to be split out against multiple different jobs, which in MYOB is not so bad because we can select the different jobs but then when we bring it across to here we've had to start adding in the comments just 'multi job' and then we can't really allocate it without adding it in, deleting it, adding it in which is the 'work-a-round' but the time that it would take to do that would be ridiculous

Duncan: oh for sure! I mean, just looking at the one that Devon's looking at now on the screen there's 140x bills, if you had to split that across 2x jobs that would take a lot of time so I completely understand not wanting to do that

Ben: yeah so we're running into like a I can create multiple jobs, say like we've got 14x disability houses and they're all physically different addresses so we've created separate jobs for them, because we're not going to get materials delivered to one site and then split it into 24x units or something they're actually going to be what's delivered for that job. And then the other problem too is that we get say our carpenters, they'll invoice just once a week and even for this, it might be for 2x different stages in just one job and I can't split that between the jobs so I've kind of got to keep things as consolidated as I can in the one job and just create a whole heap of different tasks. So we've played around sort of creating unit 1 and these tasks which have these sort of sub-tasks that we're dependent on - it's just the data entry side of it, I can't get it into the system quick enough

Duncan: If you could, and there isn't a way at the moment, so it's just sort of a theoretical question then, if you could split invoices across multiple jobs really easily, like as if you just import it and choose what charges go to what job - if that was the case then you could just do multiple jobs that have the same tasks right? using a template or something

Ben: Yeah, yep. I mean that would mean that it would then run sort of in line with what MYOB does. See that's the other thing, because of how it's tracking that we haven't actually been able to use the cost tracking side of NextMinute at this point because we can't get the invoices in from MYOB, everything goes into the in-tray, gets entered into MYOB so that we can pay the bills but anything that's on a multiple job level doesn't come across so these figures that you see here that are 'unbilled' are basically because we can't do anything with the finance side so it's more time tracking and the rest of it is just us playing around with it trying to add as much of it as possible and we find that because we've got so many multi jobs we're having to use the comment section box to define which job they're going to as well it means that you end up with a lot of error with where you're allocating them so it's not flowing for us in a document process so it's sort of one step at a time trying to figure something out. The biggest thing that's killing us is the scheduling at the moment but then I start doing what you guys just did and think well why not just create multiple jobs? this is a problem, we just keep getting cornered by restrictions basically.

Duncan: I think Bens explained it pretty well on what he's trying to achieve that really helps, and we've kind of identified two of the bottlenecks right, which is:

1. if you could split the invoices we could just do it that way and it saves a lot of headache

2. Having a nice way to import tasks I suppose would be good - but even then, if you have a job with thousands of tasks it's not going to be a fun time for anyone to manage purely because of how busy that would look as you can imagine. Even if it got in there nice and easy, just scrolling through that list would not be fun

Ben: Yeah I mean that's where the initial setup we have to put in dates & whatnot and dependencies but if we could get into the system, I've been playing around with the supervisor with his phone there while I've been trying to add tasks and stuff and that sort of gives him a list of if it goes down to a day level, it gives him a list of what he's meant to be getting the guys to do today so even if it was just at his level and not sending those direct tasks out yet, it would give him a lot more than ah, and even with that Tatura job for example I was able to go okay, the structural steel didn't turn up when it was meant to due to a design issue so we dragged that down and it dragged all of the dependencies down as well which was great, but I sort of want to be able to scale that up and I can't do that without some way of being able to get the data in quicker.

Devon: could you add the tasks as per date range maybe? (not all at once) It would be slow adding the tasks but at least there wouldn't be the urgency to get them all loaded in against the job right now sort of deal

Ben: Our business strategy is that at the start of the job we have to map it all out anyway, and then we have to submit a work plan to the consultants to get approved so that they can see we're going to meet our timelines and that side of it so ideally you have it all in there so that you can dump it out. Like I said, it worked great for the smaller job but it's just not able to cope for the detail we need for jobs with multiple addresses or units sort of thing.

Devon: Alright, so I think we've got enough information from you now to see where you're hitting a wall so pretty much we'll just go back to the drawing board and chat with our management and hopefully we'll come back with some form of work-a-round or solution - so me & Duncan will be in touch with you

Duncan: Thanks again Ben, like Devon said we'll just have a chat. We really appreciate you explaining things to us like these conversations are great for that - it really helps us get our head around what you're trying to do and how you want to do it

Ben: Yeah, what I'll do too is I've got um, we sort of mapped out in the aim of what we wanted to get out of it too because we're running MYOB out of a software package so I'll flick that through to you guys. Just have a look at it and it will give you an idea of what we're baseline trying to achieve outside of our accounting package I suppose

Devon: Awesome - thank you so much and thank you for spending the time with us and hopefully we'll chat soon!

@Duncan Geradts nice one getting Bruce over the line mate! Can you please when you've got a moment, update the deal fields 'industry', 'using nextminute for', 'qualification score' and 'basic setup checklist'? Also 'max users' at 2, is this right?

23 May

said if we have compliance then give her a call and they'll re look at it . fergus isnt that great - next step would be moving to a larger software provider but it has to have compliance.


13 March -

Summary
Ben Peet and Mathew Deitz discussed system setup progress, current testing, onboarding support, and upcoming features. Ben has connected Xero, tested bills and expenditure, and sees strong advantages over previous software. The discussion highlighted flexible job templates, staged onboarding, and future enhancements like forms, checklists, and supplier integrations.

Key Notes

  • Ben has connected NextMinute to Xero and is testing bills, POs, and expenditure tracking.

  • He noted automatic bill import as a major improvement over his previous system.

  • Customisable forms, checklists, and file folder organisation are expected within the next month.

  • Onboarding support will be tailored to Ben’s priorities with flexible scheduling.

  • Job templates can be easily customised for different project sizes and workflows.

Topics Discussed

System Setup & Testing

  • Xero integration completed.

  • Testing purchase orders, bills, and expenditure allocation.

  • Recent improvements to expenditure assignment discussed.

Upcoming Features

  • Custom forms and checklists launching soon.

  • Planned supplier integrations (eg. Bunnings).

  • Improved file management with structured folders for plans, safety docs, etc.

Onboarding & Support

  • Onboarding to be staged, starting with business setup and labour rates.

  • Ongoing support for invoicing, payroll, and workflows based on priorities.

  • Sessions can be scheduled flexibly around Ben’s availability.

Job Templates & Flexibility

  • Job templates can be created and edited for different project types.

  • Easy adjustments for measurements, materials, tasks, and costs.

  • Templates can be pulled into quotes and jobs, then customised per project.

• No job management system

  • Using Excel to track jobs and tasks

  • Using Xero for accounting only

  • No central system for:

    • Quotes

    • Job tracking

    • Staff accountability

    • Costing or profitability

Pain Points

  • Manual admin across multiple tools

  • High risk of errors and missed information

  • No visibility on:

    • Job status

    • Labour and material costs

    • Job profitability

  • No structured workflow from quote → job → invoice

What Was Shown

Mathew positioned NextMinute as a single operational system to replace spreadsheets and improve control.

Quoting

  • Build professional quotes

  • Convert quotes into jobs and job cards

Job Management

  • Track job progress

  • Assign tasks to staff

  • Job cards for office and field visibility

Task & Staff Management

  • Assign work to technicians

  • Staff receive notifications

Xero Integration

  • Sync invoices and financials

  • Reduce double handling

Materials & Suppliers

  • Live supplier price lists

  • More accurate quotes and better margin control

Invoicing & Purchase Orders

  • Generate invoices from quotes

  • Track and manage supplier invoices

Value Positioning

  • One system instead of Excel + Xero + manual tracking

  • Better job planning = stronger accountability and profitability

  • Accurate data improves:

    • Labour tracking

    • Material costing

    • Reporting and margin visibility

Interest Level

  • Mick is most interested in quoting as the entry point

  • Full CRM not required right now

  • Sees value in quote → job tracking workflows

Commercials & Support

  • Monthly subscription model explained

  • Pricing varies by user role

  • Discounts available for larger teams

  • Unlimited training and support included

Next Steps

  • Mick to gather internal info before starting a trial

  • Mathew to keep in touch

  • Mick to provide feedback on:

    • Compliance

    • Safety and operational features

• Currently using Tradify; previously used Servicem8

  • Key priority: Simple, easy-to-use timesheets for field staff, supplier bills integrated from Xero into NextMinute & Strong reporting capabilities

  • Jobs go for months at a time usually

Billing Workflow Feedback:

  • Liked ServiceMate’s feature where:

    • Unique email address converts emailed/photo invoices directly into bills

    • Could forward same invoice to Xero

    • Bills could be split across multiple jobs automatically via job code

  • Concern: In NextMinute, bills must first go into Xero, then be allocated to multiple jobs manually

  • This workflow difference noted as a limitation compared to ServiceMate

Pain Points with Tradeify:

  • Limited visibility on:

    • What’s been invoiced to date

    • What’s been ordered via purchase orders

  • Frustrated with lack of strong job tracking & cost visibility

Strong Interest In:

  • Job tracking & back costing reports

  • Better financial oversight across projects

Business Model:

  • Electrical company focused on large servicing work for other companies

  • Works with a lot of machinery

  • Wants to track:

    • Individual machines

    • Service dates

    • Usage history

    • Notes & photos

    • Costs

  • Plan discussed:

    • Create master job (e.g., “Machines”)

    • Each machine as a task

    • Add notes, photos, charges per machine

    • Invoice clients based on machine usage

App & Development:

  • Excited about the new NextMinute app

  • Keen on checklists & forms functionality

  • Wants to ensure he’s noted as interested in early access / rollout

Team Details:

  • 3 users

  • Based in Australia

    • James: Gold Coast

    • Foreman: Victoria

Overall Sentiment:
Positive conversation. Strong interest in reporting, job tracking, and future feature roadmap. Main hesitation revolves around supplier bill workflow compared to ServiceMate but keen for us to send through video on back costing and to make a comment that we are committing to checklist/forms dev work.

18 March - said meeting on wednesday - call to check in on what questions came up


Angus Interiors Current Setup

  • Based in Christchurch, 20 staff members, $6M annual turnover

  • Two project managers currently using Project Manager (US-based software)

    • Fees increased significantly with exchange rate impact

    • Underutilizing available features

  • Existing systems:

    • Site Connect for health & safety (subscription expires August)

    • Xero for accounting

    • iPayroll for payroll

  • Looking to streamline multiple systems into one platform

Key Requirements Assessment

  • Labor hour tracking against jobs/stages (watched 40-minute tutorial)

  • Profitability analysis on every job to monitor margins

    • Currently manual process: timesheets → Xero → profit/loss reports

    • Need PMs to see job tracking live as projects progress

  • Payment claims/progress payments functionality

  • Integration with iPayroll (can export CSV from NextMinute)

  • Gantt charts for project planning

  • Simple interface for “dinosaur” workers who are tech-averse

NextMinute Capabilities Demonstrated

  • Real-time job profitability tracking with weekly reporting

  • Two-way Xero integration for bills and payments

  • Mobile timesheet entry with voice-to-text capability

  • Photo capture for variations with time/date stamps

  • Role-based access (different button visibility by responsibility level)

  • Construction plans accessible on mobile devices

  • Variation management workflow with approval process

  • Forms and checklists for basic safety requirements

  • Dedicated NZ-based support person (Duncan) assigned per business

Pricing & Next Steps

  • Standard pricing: $519/month, discounted to $414/month (25% off for 15+ users)

  • $1,000 onboarding fee waived until March 31st

  • 10-day trial period offered

  • Trial setup: add one current job with stages, 3-4 staff members for timesheet testing

  • Away period: 2 weeks in office, then 7-8 days at Robertson wedding

  • Will check with Hierarchy (current NextMinute user) for feedback

  • Need to send quote and staff emails for trial setup

• Brayden Habkouk runs contracting company focusing on commercial projects and insurance work

  • Current challenges:

    • Need better job tracking and costing system

    • Boys’ timesheets inaccurate (“I think I did about six hours” vs actual eight hours)

    • Can’t identify where money is lost on jobs

    • Safety compliance requirements (SWMS documentation, photo uploads)

  • Job duration varies: few days to few weeks

  • Bigger jobs often staged (frame first, return later for spikes/different materials)

  • Uses QuickBooks for accounting

NextMinute Software Demonstration

  • Key features shown:

    • Job stage breakdown (hardscape, softscape, fencing) vs single job-level tracking

    • Real-time back costing reports showing estimated vs actual hours per stage

    • Mobile app simplicity: timesheets → select day → remember last job → add hours/photos/materials on one screen

    • Customizable user permissions (can remove buttons per user level)
